# News

### Pioneering Interoperable Quantum Key Distribution Demonstrated at NaQCI.fi with evolutionQ’s BasejumpQDN
July 16, 2025
_Independent, cross-vendor key management critical for crypto resiliency in quantum secure networking_
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/interoperable-quantum-key-distribution-demonstrated-with-evolutionq-basejumpqdn)[View Article](https://www.evolutionq.com/company/news)
![ETSI launches new standard for Quantum-Safe Hybrid Key Exchanges to secure future post-quantum encryption](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/67e432d4985240bb7e363ae6_etsinews.png)
### ETSI launches new standard for Quantum-Safe Hybrid Key Exchanges to secure future post-quantum encryption
"Today, ETSI announces the launch of its post-quantum security standard to guarantee the protection of critical data and communications in the future. The specification [“Efficient Quantum-Safe Hybrid Key Exchanges with Hidden Access Policies”](https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_ts/104000_104099/104015/01.01.01_60/ts_104015v010101p.pdf) (ETSI TS 104 015) has been developed to enhance security mechanisms, ensuring that only authorized users with the correct permissions can access sensitive data to decrypt them.
The new ETSI specification defines a scheme for Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) with Access Control (KEMAC), called Covercrypt, that ensure both pre-quantum and post-quantum security through hybridization. This means that encryption remains secure against both current threats and future quantum computing capabilities, offering a seamless transition to a more advanced cryptographic landscape. Namely, session keys will be locked based on user-attributes, kept anonymous and any user having attributes fulfilling the encapsulation policy will be able to retrieve the session keys, while those who are not authorized will not be able to. For instance, while an IT department can define who enters applications, the ETSI KEMAC standard helps to determine who can decrypt the data inside those applications through a specific access policy."
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/etsi-launches-new-standard-for-quantum-safe-hybrid-key-exchanges-to-secure-future-post-quantum-encryption)[View Article](https://www.etsi.org/newsroom/press-releases/2513-etsi-launches-new-standard-for-quantum-safe-hybrid-key-exchanges-to-secure-future-post-quantum-encryption)
![Promise of quantum computing requires new forms of protection](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/67b4ac6aee1c090e6ab0608d_global%20and%20mail.png)
### Promise of quantum computing requires new forms of protection
February 18, 2025
According to a KPMG [survey](https://kpmg.com/xx/en/our-insights/ai-and-technology/quantum-and-cybersecurity.html), 60 per cent of large corporations in Canada believe it’s only a matter of time before cybercriminals use the power of quantum to decrypt and disrupt current cybersecurity protocols.
...
“There’s a number of us, including me and my company and others around the world saying, ‘Okay, how can we use those old-school methods [with] 21st-century know-how [to] provide additional resilience?’” Mr. Mosca asks.
‍
QKD as a solution
Quantum computing also promises solutions. With Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), keys can be distributed through an optical wire and photons with some quantum mechanical properties. QKD is already in use. But the industry is still in its early days even as the quantum threat rapidly approaches.
“The idea that you could have a major algorithmic advance break all of your algorithmic methods is not speculative … it has happened,” Mr. Mosca says. “Let’s not hide under a rock, [let’s] prepare and then boldly move forward and leverage quantum and AI and anything else that comes along.”
‍
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/promise-of-quantum-computing-requires-new-forms-of-protection)[View Article](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/business/article-promise-of-quantum-computing-requires-new-forms-of-protection/)
![Demystifying Quantum Computing, its Potential and Threats](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/6797cbfc5975f1cb7f6188b7_AdobeStock_394765601-768x423.jpeg)
### Demystifying Quantum Computing, its Potential and Threats
January 27, 2025
Recent expert estimates suggest that quantum computers will threaten existing cybersecurity protocols sooner than previously thought. Organizations without a plan for a quantum-safe migration could be at risk of data breaches and other cyber attacks. 
This webinar features Dr. Michele Mosca from evolutionQ, a leading provider of quantum-safe cybersecurity solutions, who will discuss the findings of the latest [Quantum Threat Timeline Report](https://globalriskinstitute.org/publication/2024-quantum-threat-timeline-report/), what they mean for financial institutions relying on traditional encryption protocols, and how organizations are preparing for the transition to quantum-safe cryptography.
**Presenter:**
  * **Michele Mosca** , _Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer, evolutionQ Inc., and Professor in the Department of Combinatorics and Optimization, University of Waterloo_
  * **Vanda Vicars** , _Executive Consultant, Global Risk Institute_


‍
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/demystifying-quantum-computing-its-potential-and-threats)[View Article](https://globalriskinstitute.org/event/cyber-risk-implications-of-the-current-quantum-computing-timeline/)
![Quantum Threat Timeline Report 2024](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/6797c9fda96ba9953e524fcf_Quantum%20Threatimeline%202024%20-%20news%20website.png)
### Quantum Threat Timeline Report 2024
January 27, 2025
This report, collaboratively produced by GRI and evolutionQ—a leader in quantum-safe cybersecurity solutions—draws on insights from 32 global experts to explore the current state of quantum computing and its implications for cybersecurity. Now in its sixth edition for GRI, the report reveals that the threat posed by quantum computing may be closer than previously anticipated.
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/quantum-threat-timeline-report-2024)[View Article](https://globalriskinstitute.org/publication/2024-quantum-threat-timeline-report/)
![evolutionQ v Super Threat](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/667ee91d07f75fcf14a33191_batman.jpg)

### evolutionQ engaged by Bank of Canada to contribute to research on quantum-safe technologies for digital currencies
August 30, 2023
evolutionQ is contributing to a Bank of Canada research project involving quantum-safe cybersecurity for greenfield digital currencies. The open-source code accelerates quantum-safe technology development, supporting the response to evolving cryptographic challenges.
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/evolutionq-engaged-by-bank-of-canada-to-contribute-to-research-on-quantum-safe-technologies-for-digital-currencies)[View Article](https://www.newswire.ca/news-releases/evolutionq-engaged-by-bank-of-canada-to-contribute-to-research-on-quantum-safe-technologies-for-digital-currencies-873311376.html)

### evolutionQ's flagship software, BasejumpQDN™, used by Nokia and Proximus for Europe’s first live hybrid quantum encryption key trial 
June 20, 2023
BasejumpQDN™, the flagship software from evolutionQ, was part of a collaborative effort with Nokia and IDQuantique to enable Proximus, a Belgian telecommunications leader, to establish a quantum-safe optical network connection using quantum key distribution (QKD). The live demonstration represents a significant milestone in the development of quantum key technologies and highlights the future of network security in the era of quantum computing.
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/basejumpqdn-nokia-proximus-qkd)[View Article](https://www.nokia.com/about-us/news/releases/2023/06/20/nokia-and-proximus-demonstrate-future-of-network-security-with-europes-first-live-hybrid-quantum-encryption-key-trial/)

### evolutionQ Selected by World Economic Forum as a Technology Pioneer for 2023 Cohort
June 21, 2023
evolutionQ was selected to join a cohort of the 100 most promising Technology Pioneers from 31 economies on six continents. They join a dedicated group of change-makers in key fields such as cybersecurity, sustainability, climate change and healthcare.

### Quantum cyber security: Keeping secrets safe in the quantum age
June 7, 2021
Thank you to our valued partner the National Research Council Canada / Conseil national de recherches Canada for their leadership in proactively tackling the cyber threats posed by quantum computers and investing in technology like evolutionQ’s BasejumpQDN.
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/quantum-cyber-security)[View Article](https://nrc.canada.ca/en/stories/quantum-cyber-security-keeping-secrets-safe-quantum-age)
![evolutionQ Introduces BasejumpQDN™ Quantum Delivery Network Technology for Secure Networks](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/63f05800e2860c108b432f33_20210504-basejump-announce.webp)
### evolutionQ Introduces BasejumpQDN™ Quantum Delivery Network Technology for Secure Networks
May 4, 2021
evolutionQ announces the availability of Quantum Delivery Network (QDN) software product BasejumpQDN™ to provide quantum-safe security services to high-speed networks. A quantum-safe software product that allows organizations who are building a network based on Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) devices to easily deploy and cost-effectively manage quantum technologies throughout their network.
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/evolutionq-basejumpqdn-announcement)[View Article](https://www.evolutionq.com/company/news)
![evolutionQ publishes research report on Quantum Random Number Generators](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/63f058092ad2dc810330909f_qrng-report-title.webp)
### evolutionQ publishes research report on Quantum Random Number Generators
January 13, 2021
evolutionQ published a research report on Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs) that is freely available for download on the evolutionQ website. The vendor-neutral research report covers market use cases and technical descriptions of the various types of QRNGs available in the market.
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/qrng-report-announcement)[View Article](https://www.evolutionq.com/company/news)
![Canada Space Agency awards funding to evolutionQ for space-based QKD network R&D](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/63ef0996726f31b9968ba679/63f05810b50e55433fe02f29_20200804-CSA-funding-announcement-csa-logo.webp)
### Canada Space Agency awards funding to evolutionQ for space-based QKD network R&D
August 10, 2020
evolutionQ was awarded a Space Technology Development Program (STDP) contribution by the CSA to develop solutions to advance satellite-based secure quantum communication services.
[View Announcement](https://www.evolutionq.com/news-releases/space-based-qkd-network)[View Article](https://www.evolutionq.com/company/news)

ETSI launches new standard for Quantum-Safe Hybrid Key Exchanges to secure future post-quantum encryption

"Today, ETSI announces the launch of its post-quantum security standard to guarantee the protection of critical data and communications in the future. The specification “Efficient Quantum-Safe Hybrid Key Exchanges with Hidden Access Policies” (ETSI TS 104 015) has been developed to enhance security mechanisms, ensuring that only authorized users with the correct permissions can access sensitive data to decrypt them.

The new ETSI specification defines a scheme for Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) with Access Control (KEMAC), called Covercrypt, that ensure both pre-quantum and post-quantum security through hybridization. This means that encryption remains secure against both current threats and future quantum computing capabilities, offering a seamless transition to a more advanced cryptographic landscape. Namely, session keys will be locked based on user-attributes, kept anonymous and any user having attributes fulfilling the encapsulation policy will be able to retrieve the session keys, while those who are not authorized will not be able to. For instance, while an IT department can define who enters applications, the ETSI KEMAC standard helps to determine who can decrypt the data inside those applications through a specific access policy."

‍

QKD as a solution

Quantum computing also promises solutions. With Quantum Key Distribution (QKD), keys can be distributed through an optical wire and photons with some quantum mechanical properties. QKD is already in use. But the industry is still in its early days even as the quantum threat rapidly approaches.

“The idea that you could have a major algorithmic advance break all of your algorithmic methods is not speculative … it has happened,” Mr. Mosca says. “Let’s not hide under a rock, [let’s] prepare and then boldly move forward and leverage quantum and AI and anything else that comes along.”
